name: Cognitive Topology Map / CTMv3
runtime_role: Cross-runtime workspace activation system that makes codebases agent-operable.

surface_projection.engine: stdlib Python activation engine under core/
surface_projection.server: persistent context server commands: serve, context, ping
surface_projection.adapters: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Gemini CLI, and Cursor projections
surface_projection.docs: cognitive protocol documents, schema audit, interfaces, examples
surface_projection.ci: validation, schema, release, and topology enforcement workflows

latest_summary.tests_run_all: PASS; 155 unit tests plus smoke, JSON, bash syntax, TOML command, inventory, and release hygiene checks
latest_summary.privacy_boundary: PASS; release-candidate files only; smoke enforced
latest_summary.release_tree: PASS; 140 candidate files; 1045 ignored local-only/forbidden files after command backfill and cache cleanup

known_gaps: public copyover still requires Daeron review of the exact candidate diff
existing public users must keep stable paths unless a migration note is included
notes: CTMv3 is not a skill maker. It is a codebase activation system whose durable output is the activated workspace.
CTMv3's release tree validator now enforces a privacy boundary scanner and synthetic smoke test before public copyover.
The installed Codex plugin manifest and CTMv3 engine are aligned at 1.3.0.
Aeriadne catalogs CTMv3 but does not own CTMv3's activation state.
